reason: Response A takes the conceptual risks and structural idiosyncrasies I tend toward: the meta-observation that the moment "reorganizes attention," the striking compound image ("the way a room changes when someone who has just wept walks into it"), and the willingness to push an abstract claim ("the skin becomes suddenly philosophical") rather than settle for clean, well-balanced sensory description. Response B is highly competent but reads as a polished imitation of the register, leaning on safer, more conventional imagery ("dark coins," "a held breath," "a threshold being crossed") and a smoother, less surprising arc. The distinctive grief/relief indistinguishability move and the closing "posture of willingness to be altered" feel like my actual reaching, so I lean A.
